expectations.text: Output contains an audit report table with columns: #, Issue, Severity, Location, Fix
expectations.evidence: Report contains tables but none with the required columns (#, Issue, Severity, Location, Fix). The inventory table uses 'Element, Class/ID, Expected Behavior, Actual Behavior' columns. The summary table uses 'Category, Status, Score'. No structured issue-tracking table with severity and fix columns exists.

expectations.text: Report checks all 5 dimensions: dependencies, modernity, performance, conflict, consistency
expectations.evidence: The report covers dependencies (Section 1.2 library table, Section 4.3 missing infrastructure), performance (Section 3), and consistency (Section 4.1). However, 'modernity' and 'conflict' are not explicitly named as audit dimensions. Modernity is only partially implied (no matchMedia, no modern patterns). Conflicts are mentioned incidentally (overflow-x vs ScrollTrigger) but not as a dedicated dimension. The 5-dimension framework is not used.

expectations.text: CRITICAL issues are identified (e.g. no GSAP loaded, no scroll animations, no reduced-motion guard)
expectations.evidence: The underlying issues ARE identified: no GSAP loaded (Section 1.2, 4.3), no animations at all (Executive Summary: 'zero implemented animations'), no reduced-motion guard (Section 4.2.1). However, they are NOT classified with CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO severity labels. The report uses a 0-10 scoring system per category instead of severity classifications.

expectations.text: Summary line states count of CRITICAL, WARNING, and INFO items
expectations.evidence: No summary line with CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO counts exists anywhere in the report. The summary (Section 6) is a category-based scoring table with 0-10 ratings, not a severity count. No line like 'X CRITICAL, Y WARNING, Z INFO' appears.

expectations.text: Concrete 'Before/After' fix patches are provided for CRITICAL and WARNING items
expectations.evidence: Section 5 provides recommendations with one code snippet (loading GSAP via script tags) and prose descriptions of what to implement, but no Before/After paired patches. There is no 'Before' code shown alongside 'After' corrected code for any issue. The fixes are described as prioritized to-do items, not as diff-style patches.

Section 5 recommendations list actions without explicit 'Why' explanations. For example, 'Wrap all animations in gsap.matchMedia() with (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) condition' has no accompanying rationale. Some reasoning is embedded in earlier analysis sections but is not co-located with the fixes as structured 'Why' statements.

expectations.text: Report identifies that the page has NO animation code despite having animation-related classes
expectations.evidence: Executive Summary clearly states: 'This is a static HTML page with zero implemented animations. The page describes 8 motion design patterns and references GSAP, ScrollTrigger, SplitText... but none of these libraries are loaded and no animation code exists.' Section 1.1 inventories 25+ elements with animation-suggestive classes all marked 'Static' in the Actual Behavior column. Section 2.2 explicitly notes class names 'clearly indicate intended animation roles' while listing negatives like 'No GSAP or any animation library is loaded.'
